spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the headline Clipboardchange event - Sticky activation / Permission check surface (clipboard-read, clipboard-apis, clipboard-event-clipboardchange); residual gaps in spec vocabulary: PSA.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, eventListener); controls include "Subscribe to clipboardchange"; "Write a fresh payload to clipboard"; "Clear history" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the required canonical ChromeStatus self-heal link https://chromestatus.com/feature/5163741220044800.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on clipboard-differ
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: clipboard-differ covers the happy path of Clipboardchange event - Sticky activation / Permission check and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions. ChromeStatus reference link resolved.
